evals.prompt: Summarize this YouTube video for me: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ
evals.expected_output: A structured digest with TL;DR, key takeaways, topic timeline, and notable quotes in clean Markdown with video metadata header.
expectations: Runs fetch_transcript.py to extract transcript and metadata from the URL
Output includes a TL;DR section with 1-2 sentence summary
Output includes a Key Takeaways section with 3-7 bullet points
Output includes a Topic Timeline table with timestamps
Output includes video metadata header (title, channel, duration)
Output is saved as a .md file

evals.prompt: I found this long lecture on transformer architectures: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EXAMPLE_ID. I only have 5 minutes -- is it worth watching for someone doing NLP fine-tuning?
evals.expected_output: A triage-mode digest with TL;DR, filtered takeaways weighted toward NLP fine-tuning, and a clear worth-watching verdict.
expectations: Produces a TL;DR that directly addresses whether the video is worth watching
Key Takeaways are filtered toward NLP fine-tuning relevance
Core Assertions section identifies main technical claims with timestamps
Includes a relevance verdict (worth watching / skip)
Does not hallucinate content not present in the transcript

evals.prompt: Digest this video and save it to my Obsidian vault at ~/vault/YouTube/: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EXAMPLE_ID2. Tag it with 'machine-learning' and 'tutorial'.
evals.expected_output: An Obsidian-compatible Markdown file with YAML frontmatter containing title, channel, date, duration, url, and specified tags, saved to the vault path.
expectations: Output file has YAML frontmatter with title, channel, date, duration, url fields
Frontmatter includes tags array with 'machine-learning' and 'tutorial'
File is saved to ~/vault/YouTube/ with a meaningful filename
Body contains the full digest structure (TL;DR, Key Takeaways, Timeline, etc.)
Frontmatter includes 'type: youtube-digest'

evals.prompt:
I have two videos about climate change policy. Digest both and tell me where they agree and disagree:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CLIMATE_1
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CLIMATE_2
evals.expected_output: Individual digests for each video followed by a comparison section with agreements, disagreements, and unique contributions.
expectations: Produces a separate digest for each video with all standard sections
Includes a comparison section after the individual digests
Comparison identifies at least one agreement and one disagreement
Each video's claims are attributed correctly and not mixed up
Comparison references specific timestamps from each video

evals.prompt: https://youtu.be/SHORT_ID -- what are the main claims? I want to fact-check some for a blog post.
evals.expected_output: A digest with detailed Core Assertions section, each claim categorized and timestamped.
expectations: Handles the shortened youtu.be URL format correctly
Core Assertions section is more detailed than standard
Each claim is categorized (opinion, anecdote, cited statistic, etc.)
Timestamps are included for each claim
Includes a disclaimer that claims should be independently verified
